div.p: Segments are the fundamental building blocks of HL7 v2+ messages. Each segment contains a logically related group of data fields. A segment is identified by a unique three-character code (e.g., MSH, PID, OBX).

ul.li: The fields contained in the segment, in order
The data type of each field
Value set bindings for coded fields

div.p: The following segments are included in this demo build as representative examples:

tr.td: Present in every HL7 v2 message. Contains metadata about the message including sending/receiving applications, message type, and encoding characters.
Contains information about the trigger event that initiated the message.
Contains patient demographic information including identifiers, name, date of birth, sex, race, address, and phone numbers.
Contains visit-specific information such as assigned patient location, attending doctor, visit number, and admission type.
Contains information about the patient's next of kin or associated parties.
Contains a single clinical observation or result, including the observation identifier, value, units, and reference range.
Contains patient diagnosis information including diagnosis code, type, and description.
Contains patient allergy information including allergen type, code, severity, and reaction.

p.i: The full v2+ specification includes 192 segment definitions. This demo build includes a representative subset to demonstrate the publication pipeline.
